docs.text: Scripts Canonical CLI script references for fixture generation, optimizer runs, backend checks, cache workflows, and analytics plotting. generate_a_strategy_fixtures : regenerate canonical persistent fixtures. run_exhaustive_strategy_optimizer : exhaustive optimizer CLI. run_beam_strategy_optimizer : beam optimizer CLI. compare_strategy_evaluation_backends : backend consistency validation. validate_strategy_cache_behavior : strict cache validation suite. rebuild_evaluation_cache : cache rebuild and sweep tool. plot_analytics_family_curves : analytical family curve plotting.
docs.title: Overview

docs.text: compare_strategy_evaluation_backends.py Purpose Compare strategy evaluation backends via unified evaluator dispatcher. CLI usage python scripts/validate/compare_strategy_evaluation_backends.py --help CLI options Option Type Default Required Choices Description --exact-tolerance float 1e-12 False Maximum allowed absolute difference for exact comparisons. --json-output Path None False Optional path to write JSON report. --mc-tolerance float 0.03 False Maximum allowed absolute difference for MC comparisons. --n-eval-samples int 20000 False MC evaluation samples for dense_mc/procedural_mc. --n-train-samples int 10000 False MC training samples for dense_mc/procedural_mc. --p-rules float 0.5 0.8 1.0 False Rule probabilities to evaluate (default: 0.5 0.8 1.0). --quiet flag False False Reduce console output. --seed int 123 False Random seed for MC evaluators. --spec-set str tiny False tiny Named canonical spec set to validate. See also hybrid_strategy_spec strategy_evaluation

docs.text: plot_analytics_family_curves.py Purpose Plot closed-form analytical win-rate curves for representative hybrid strategy families. CLI usage python scripts/compare/plot_analytics_family_curves.py --help CLI options Option Type Default Required Choices Description --dpi int 150 False f'PNG resolution. Default: {DEFAULT_DPI}' --n2-values int 4 8 16 False n2 values to plot. Default: 4 8 16 --optimal-search-summary-output Path None False Optional CSV path (or directory) for p_rule,best_success,best_candidate_name summary. --output-dir Path DEFAULT_OUTPUT_DIR False f'Directory to save PNG figures. Default: {DEFAULT_OUTPUT_DIR}' --search-families str majority pyramid horizontal vertical False majority pyramid horizontal vertical Families to include in optimal analytical search. --search-k-box-values int None False Optional search k_box values. Defaults by plot n2: n2=4 -> 1 3; n2=8 -> 1 3 7; n2=16 -> 1 3 7 15. --search-n2-values int None False Optional search n2 values. Default: same n2 as current plot. --show flag False False Display figures interactively after saving. --show-optimal-search flag False False Overlay optimal analytical search envelope on each figure. See also hybrid_strategy_analytics hybrid_strategy_spec strategy_search

docs.text: Source Modules Canonical source modules under src/ssf used by the evaluator, search, optimizer, and cache flow. hybrid_strategy_spec : canonical strategy specification and naming. hybrid_strategy_fixtures : canonical dense fixture construction and serialization. hybrid_strategy_procedural : procedural strategy implementation matching dense semantics. hybrid_strategy_analytics : closed-form analytical evaluator backend. strategy_evaluation : backend-agnostic evaluator dispatcher. strategy_cache : evaluation cache schema and replacement policy. strategy_search : legal spec enumeration and exhaustive ranking utilities. strategy_optimizer : exhaustive and beam optimizer orchestration. bob_exhaustive : dense exhaustive B-side evaluator backend. bob_mc : dense Monte Carlo B-side evaluator backend. bob_mc_procedural : procedural Monte Carlo B-side evaluator backend.
docs.title: Overview

docs.title: Source Modules

docs.text: bob_exhaustive Purpose Exact dense B-side evaluator over canonical dense fixtures. Role in canonical architecture Implements the dense exhaustive backend used by strategy_evaluation.evaluate_candidate in dense_exhaustive mode. Input contract Strategy payload must contain canonical dense tables ( n2 , k_box , f_tables , comm / comm_table ). Table shapes must satisfy the dense fixture contract. p_rule must be in [0, 1] . Evaluation mode relation This module is the engine behind dense_exhaustive . Exact vs approximate Exact: enumerates restricted Bob responses for allowed subset policy/box limit. Cache and evaluator relationship This module does not own cache policy. Cache read/write is handled by strategy_evaluation and strategy_cache . Must not do No strategy-name shortcuts. No expected-score shortcuts. No fixture-name assumptions. See also strategy_evaluation strategy_cache bob_mc bob_mc_procedural
docs.title: bob_exhaustive

docs.text: bob_mc Purpose Approximate dense B-side evaluator using Monte Carlo over canonical dense fixtures. Role in canonical architecture Implements the dense Monte Carlo backend used by strategy_evaluation.evaluate_candidate in dense_mc mode. Input contract Strategy payload must contain canonical dense tables ( n2 , k_box , f_tables , comm / comm_table ). Sampling parameters ( n_train_samples , n_eval_samples , confidence , seed ) must be valid. p_rule must be in [0, 1] . Evaluation mode relation This module is the engine behind dense_mc . Exact vs approximate Approximate: two-phase Monte Carlo (training for Bob policy selection, evaluation for score estimate). Cache and evaluator relationship This module does not own cache policy. Cache read/write is handled by strategy_evaluation and strategy_cache . Must not do No strategy-name shortcuts. No expected-score shortcuts. No fixture-name assumptions. See also strategy_evaluation strategy_cache bob_exhaustive bob_mc_procedural
docs.title: bob_mc

docs.text: bob_mc_procedural Purpose Approximate procedural B-side evaluator using Monte Carlo against the procedural A-side contract. Role in canonical architecture Implements the procedural Monte Carlo backend used by strategy_evaluation.evaluate_candidate in procedural_mc mode. Input contract Strategy must expose canonical procedural API: n2 k_box measure_a(box_idx, field, out_a_prefix) -> int comm(field, out_a) -> int Outputs from measure_a and comm must be binary. Sampling parameters and p_rule must be valid. Evaluation mode relation This module is the engine behind procedural_mc . Exact vs approximate Approximate: two-phase Monte Carlo with the same high-level Bob search structure as dense MC. Cache and evaluator relationship This module does not own cache policy. Cache read/write is handled by strategy_evaluation and strategy_cache . Must not do No strategy-name shortcuts. No expected-score shortcuts. No fixture-name assumptions. See also strategy_evaluation strategy_cache hybrid_strategy_procedural bob_exhaustive bob_mc
docs.title: bob_mc_procedural

docs.text: hybrid_strategy_analytics Purpose Closed-form analytical scoring for HybridStrategySpec. Role in canonical architecture Closed-form analytical strategy scoring backend. Key dataclasses Name Purpose AnalyticalResult Key functions Name Purpose analytical_win_rate Compute the closed-form analytical win rate for a strategy spec. majority_success_probability Return the A-side win probability for a majority-vote strategy over n bits. majority_success_probability_given_not_tie Return majority success conditioned on being outside the tie region. majority_tie_probability Return the probability that a uniform random n-bit string falls in a tie. pyramid_success_probability Return the A-side win probability for a pyramid strategy. Design invariants The vertical closed-form formulas assume that majority-block correctness and pyramid routing errors combine independently through XOR double-error cancellation. Specifically: For vertical maj_pyr: each majority block is assumed to produce an independent correct/incorrect bit; the pyramid routes one block's output to the final output, and an error in both the majority and pyramid layers cancels via XOR, giving success = Pm Pp + (1-Pm) (1-Pp). For vertical pyr_maj: the pyramid selects one apex input; the apex majority then decides; the same XOR cancellation assumption applies. These formulas are marked exact=True for now pending empirical validation. See also hybrid_strategy_spec
docs.title: analytics

docs.text: Name Purpose analytical_win_rate Compute the closed-form analytical win rate for a strategy spec. majority_success_probability Return the A-side win probability for a majority-vote strategy over n bits. majority_success_probability_given_not_tie Return majority success conditioned on being outside the tie region. majority_tie_probability Return the probability that a uniform random n-bit string falls in a tie. pyramid_success_probability Return the A-side win probability for a pyramid strategy.
docs.title: Key functions

docs.text: The vertical closed-form formulas assume that majority-block correctness and pyramid routing errors combine independently through XOR double-error cancellation. Specifically: For vertical maj_pyr: each majority block is assumed to produce an independent correct/incorrect bit; the pyramid routes one block's output to the final output, and an error in both the majority and pyramid layers cancels via XOR, giving success = Pm Pp + (1-Pm) (1-Pp). For vertical pyr_maj: the pyramid selects one apex input; the apex majority then decides; the same XOR cancellation assumption applies. These formulas are marked exact=True for now pending empirical validation.

docs.text: Canonical Architecture Purpose SSF uses a single canonical strategy representation and a backend-agnostic evaluation interface so optimizers can compare strategies without depending on backend internals. Core Ownership Concern Canonical Owner Strategy schema, naming, validity hybrid_strategy_spec Dense fixture semantics hybrid_strategy_fixtures Procedural semantics hybrid_strategy_procedural Closed-form analytical model hybrid_strategy_analytics Unified evaluator dispatcher strategy_evaluation Evaluation cache and replacement policy strategy_cache Legal spec enumeration strategy_search Exhaustive and beam optimizers strategy_optimizer Canonical Evaluation Modes analytical dense_exhaustive dense_mc procedural_mc Design Invariants Canonical names are generated via candidate_name(...) only. Optimizers score candidates through the dispatcher ( evaluate_candidate(...) ). Cache keys include strategy identity, evaluation mode, and evaluator configuration. Dense and procedural implementations are equivalent strategy semantics with different runtime forms. Validation and Operations Validation and operational scripts are documented in API / scripts .
docs.title: Overview
